TimeEntry.Title: Not receiving emails
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per email received from Katy for having email receiving issue on machine CBILT12, connected to user machine. Other emails were receiving the only emails not coming from scan to email from printer. Asked user to turn off and then turn ON the printer and test the email flow. But it was still not working.

Then We checked the Konica Minolta bizhub C558 for Scan to Email functionality by accessing its web interface, enabling SMTP transmission, and setting up the correct mail server configuration. The copier’s SMTP settings were updated to use an authenticated mail relay (either SMTP2GO or Microsoft 365) with SSL/TLS encryption on port 587, and the appropriate sender credentials (scan@cbinteriors.net) were applied. We verified that the device email and network settings were correct, ensured outbound SMTP ports were open, but still user was not able to receive email .

Also verified other are receiving the email, only Katy having the issue. So will check again after 1 hour with user.

TimeEntry.Title: Not receiving emails
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the handover connected to the Katty computer..

user having scan to mail issue....

so access the printer and checked the Address store settings and also compared the setting with other address as well...

so asked the user to try to scan now...

so checked the job history and found that the job was completed....

so make her login to her email on web and checked no scam mail found....so checked for anty rule or blocked mail senders....everything was fine...

so login to the office 365 portal and run the tracert and found that the mail has been deleivered to the user....

so connect again and user confimed that she got the mails now.....hence closing the  ticket.....

TimeEntry.Title: ALERT: [Medium] Malicious activity detected on CBILT03
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received CrowdStrike alert indicating malicious activity (Impact via Inhibit System Recovery) on CBILT03 with severity Medium – Prevented.

Actions Performed:

  • Reviewed CrowdStrike detection details and verified the alert.

  • Connected to CBILT03 to investigate the flagged activity.

  • Identified and deleted the malicious file detected by CrowdStrike.

  • Ran an on-demand full system scan to confirm no additional threats were present.

  • Verified system stability and ensured no further detections were triggered.

TimeEntry.Title: ManageEngine Sync Issue – CBILT04 & CBILT01
TimeEntry.Notes:
Identified a syncing issue in ManageEngine for the devices CBILT04 and CBILT01. The systems were not properly synchronizing with the ManageEngine server, causing delays in reporting and updates.

Actions Taken:

  • Logged into the ManageEngine portal and verified the sync status for both affected machines.

  • Checked communication between the endpoint agent and the ManageEngine server.

  • Performed necessary troubleshooting steps to re-establish connectivity and resolve synchronization errors.

  • Re-initiated sync and confirmed successful communication.

  • Verified in ManageEngine that both devices are now syncing properly and reporting as expected.

Current Status:
The sync issue has been successfully resolved. Both devices (CBILT04 and CBILT01) are now syncing properly in ManageEngine.

TimeEntry.Title: Christian Brothers Interiors - Can't Open PDF's
TimeEntry.Notes:
Received an email on support from Andy Stasch regarding an issue with opening PDF files. Connected to the user’s machine CBILT07 and verified that PDF attachments from emails were opening properly. However, when attempting to open PDF files directly from Windows Explorer, the files were not opening — only showing as loading.

Checked the Task Manager to confirm if any PDF processes were stuck, but none were active. Changed the default PDF viewer to Microsoft Edge, then Chrome, and finally Adobe Acrobat, but the issue persisted. Performed a system cleanup and rebooted the machine.

After the reboot, PDF files started opening smoothly. Requested the user to check and confirm — the issue was resolved, and files were opening fine.

TimeEntry.Title: RE: Communications Site Issue
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary – Communications Site / OneDrive Access Issue

Requester: Jennifer Jenike
Email: jennifer.j@cbinteriors.net
Date Reported: October 31, 2025
Cc: Andy Stasch (andy.s@cbinteriors.net)
Technician: Parveen Biswal
Device/Service: OneDrive / Communication Site

Issue Description:

User reported being unable to access the shared drive (OneDrive Communication Site). The site appeared blank even after multiple restarts of her system.

Actions Taken:

  1. Connected remotely to the user’s system to diagnose the OneDrive access issue.

  2. Verified OneDrive configuration and synchronization status.

  3. Safely removed and unlinked the existing OneDrive account from the local machine.

  4. Cleared cached files and residual OneDrive configuration folders from the user profile.

  5. Reinstalled and reconfigured OneDrive, ensuring proper authentication and sync setup.

  6. Confirmed that the Communication Site and shared documents were visible and fully synchronized.

Resolution:

After reinstallation and reconfiguration, all OneDrive files and shared folders became accessible. Verified access and synchronization functionality with the user during the remote session.

TimeEntry.Title: Major computer issues
TimeEntry.Notes: As per request from Jason called the user and verified that there is no issue with your system or Outlook. The problem is with your home internet over Wi-Fi. We tried connecting using your work phone’s hotspot, and it worked fine but was slow. As discussed, you do not have another Wi-Fi network to test with. You rebooted your Frontier Wi-Fi and tried again, but the internet was still not working properly. We tested again using the hotspot from your work phone, and it is working now. You will test with Frontier Wi-Fi again tomorrow morning and let us know the results.

TimeEntry.Title: Major computer issues
TimeEntry.Notes:
As per the email, called the user Erica to provide remote access. She was trying to connect to her home internet, but it was not working. Assisted the user in connecting her laptop to the mobile hotspot to start the remote session.

Checked the remote system and found it was running very slow. After troubleshooting, identified that the issue was caused by DNS. Uninstalled Cisco Umbrella and restarted the system. After the restart, ran ipconfig /flushdns and asked the user to connect the system to the home internet.

The internet started working fine. Verified Outlook—working properly. Also checked internal sites, and everything was working fine for the user.

Hence, closing this ticket.

TimeEntry.Title: Asbhrook 140
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: Trust Certificate Error on Jason's Mac

As per the handover, I investigated an issue on Jason's Mac where sending emails triggered a "Trust Certificate" error for recipients due to digital signature warnings.

I connected to Jason's system, reviewed the Outlook signing configuration, and identified that automatic digital signing was enabled using a certificate not trusted by recipients.

To resolve the issue, I:

  • Accessed Outlook settings on Jason's Mac.

  • Disabled automatic digital signing of outgoing emails.

  • Verified that the trust certificate error no longer appears when Jason sends emails.

? Issue resolved and functionality confirmed.

TimeEntry.Title: Dynamic Group Creation & Huntress Learner Sync Configuration for CBI
TimeEntry.Notes: Configured a new Microsoft Entra ID Dynamic User Group specifically designed to include only licensed mailbox-enabled users. Created the group using a validated dynamic membership rule that filters for active, member-type accounts with an enabled Exchange Online service plan, ensuring only real, licensed users are included. After successfully creating and verifying the dynamic group membership logic, proceeded to integrate Huntress SAT with Microsoft Graph. In the Huntress portal, navigated to Integrations → Providers, selected the CBI tenant, and chose the newly created dynamic user group (CBI_Users) as the group to sync learners from. Enabled the option to exclude unlicensed and non-human learner identities, ensuring only properly licensed human users can be provisioned as Huntress learners. Confirmed that Use UPN as unique email address was selected to maintain identity accuracy. Activated Automatic Daily Sync so learner accounts are continuously updated based on group membership changes. Also enabled attribute sharing options (Status, First Name, Last Name, Employee ID, Department, Manager) and verified advanced settings related to learner activation/inactivation and department creation. Completed validation of the sync configuration to ensure that only authorized, licensed users will populate within Huntress SAT moving forward

TimeEntry.Title: Brandywine Remote Issues Again
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary: VPN Connection Issue (CBI - Jason)

Issue:
Jason is unable to connect to the Sophos VPN. The VPN profile was verified on the firewall and credentials were confirmed to be correct. However, the issue persists due to the requirement for a six-digit OTP linked to Jason's user account.

Actions Taken:

  • Verified the VPN profile and credentials.

  • Checked the firewall configuration to ensure the profile was correct.

  • Discussed the issue with Jason, who is currently in a meeting and unable to provide the OTP at this time.

Next Steps:

  • Follow up with Jason after his meeting to retrieve the six-digit OTP and complete the VPN connection setup.

  • Google Chrome (x64) – Versions 146.0.7680.153 / 146.0.7680.154
  • Microsoft 365 Apps for Enterprise (Current Channel, x64) – Version 2602 (Build 19725.20190)

Post validation, confirmed that all patches have been successfully applied and no high vulnerabilities remain. Both systems are now compliant as per patch management policy.

TimeEntry.Title: Brandywine Remote Issues Again
TimeEntry.Notes:
Ticket Summary:

Received an email from Jason regarding an issue accessing the CI application on the 91 VM. Jason reported that the application was failing when attempting to connect using his Guardian setup.

Verified that the credentials, connection details, and node configuration were correct. Initial troubleshooting included removing and reconfiguring the VPN connection; however, the issue persisted.

Performed additional steps including disabling and re-enabling the user account, followed by a complete uninstall and reinstall of the application on the endpoint.

After reinstallation, Jason tested access again and was able to successfully connect to the application. Functionality was confirmed, and the issue is now resolved.
